name: astrowidgets

metadata.name: astrowidgets
metadata.summary: Widgets for the Jupyter notebook and JupyterLab

documentation:
# Widgets for the Jupyter notebook and JupyterLab

```{image} http://img.shields.io/badge/powered%20by-AstroPy-orange.svg?style=flat
:alt: Powered by Astropy Badge
:target: http://www.astropy.org
```

```{image} https://github.com/astropy/astrowidgets/workflows/CI/badge.svg
:alt: CI Status
:target: https://github.com/astropy/astrowidgets/actions
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/astrowidgets/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://astrowidgets.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

`astrowidgets` aims to be a set of astronomy widgets for Jupyter Lab or Notebook,
leveraging the Astropy ecosystem. The `ImageWidget` implements the API documented at
[https://github.com/eteq/nb-astroimage-api](https://github.com/eteq/nb-astroimage-api)

**Warning**

`astrowidgets` is currently in *very* early development.  Nothing is guaranteed to work or do anything in particular
at this stage.

# Installation and Documentation

See <https://astrowidgets.readthedocs.io/en/latest/> .

# License

See `LICENSE.rst`.

# Contributing

Given the pre-alpha nature of this package, there is no established
contributing guide. Please contact the maintainers for guidance.
It is important that you follow our code of conduct; please see
`CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`.

# Changelog

See `CHANGES.rst`.

functions.name: astrowidgets.tests.test_image_widget._make_fake_ccd
documentation:
Generate a CCDData object for use with ImageWidget tests.

# Parameters

with_wcs

: If `True` the image will have a WCS attached to it,
  which is useful for some of the marker tests.

# Returns

`astropy.nddata.CCDData`

: CCD image

functions.name: astrowidgets.tests.test_image_widget.test_adding_markers_as_world_recovers_with_get_markers
documentation:
Make sure that our internal conversion from world to pixel
coordinates doesn't mess anything up.

functions.name: astrowidgets.tests.test_image_widget.test_can_add_markers_with_names
documentation: Test a few things related to naming marker sets

functions.name: astrowidgets.tests.test_image_widget.test_unknown_marker_name_error
documentation:
Regression test for https://github.com/astropy/astrowidgets/issues/97

This particular test checks that getting a marker name that
does not exist raises an error.

functions.name: astrowidgets.tests.test_image_widget.test_marker_name_has_no_marks_warning
documentation:
Regression test for https://github.com/astropy/astrowidgets/issues/97

This particular test checks that getting an empty table gives a
useful warning message.

functions.name: astrowidgets.tests.test_image_widget.test_empty_marker_name_works_with_all
documentation:
Regression test for https://github.com/astropy/astrowidgets/issues/97

This particular test checks that an empty table doesn't break
marker_name='all'. The bug only comes up if there is a coordinate
column, so use a fake image a WCS.

functions.name: astrowidgets.tests.test_image_widget.test_add_single_marker
documentation: Test a few things related to naming marker sets

modules.name: astrowidgets.core

classes.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.logger
documentation: Logger for this widget.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.pixel_offset
documentation:
An offset, typically either 0 or 1, to add/subtract to all
pixel values when going to/from the displayed image.
*In almost all situations the default value, \`\`0\`\`, is the
correct value to use.*

This value cannot be modified after initialization.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget._mouse_move_cb
documentation: Callback to display position in RA/DEC deg.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget._mouse_click_cb
documentation: Callback to handle mouse clicks.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.load_fits
documentation:
Load a FITS file into the viewer.

# Parameters

fitsorfn

: Either a file name or an HDU (*not* an HDUList).
  If file name is given, WCS in primary header is automatically
  inherited. If a single HDU is given, WCS must be in the HDU
  header.

numhdu

: Extension number of the desired HDU.
  If `None`, it is determined automatically.

memmap

: Memory mapping.
  If `None`, it is determined automatically.
def _repr_html_(self):
Show widget in Jupyter notebook.
from IPython.display import display
return display(self._widget)

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.load_nddata
documentation:
Load an `NDData` object into the viewer.

```
.. todo:: Add flag/masking support, etc.
```

# Parameters

nddata

: `NDData` with image data and WCS.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.load_array
documentation:
Load a 2D array into the viewer.

:::{note}
Use {meth}`load_nddata` for WCS support.
:::

# Parameters

arr

: 2D array.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.center_on
documentation:
Centers the view on a particular point.

# Parameters

point

: If tuple of `(X, Y)` is given, it is assumed
  to be in data coordinates.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.offset_to
documentation:
Move the center to a point that is given offset
away from the current center.

:::{note}
This is deprecated. Use {meth}`offset_by`.
:::

# Parameters

dx, dy

: Offset value. Unit is assumed based on
  `skycoord_offset`.

skycoord_offset

: If `True`, offset must be given in degrees.
  Otherwise, they are in pixel values.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.offset_by
documentation:
Move the center to a point that is given offset
away from the current center.

Parameters
----------
dx, dy : float or `~astropy.unit.Quantity`
    Offset value. Without a unit, assumed to be pixel offsets.
    If a unit is attached, offset by pixel or sky is assumed from
    the unit.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.zoom_level
documentation:
Zoom level:

* 1 means real-pixel-size.
* 2 means zoomed in by a factor of 2.
* 0.5 means zoomed out by a factor of 2.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.zoom
documentation:
Zoom in or out by the given factor.

Parameters
----------
val : int
    The zoom level to zoom the image.
    See `zoom_level`.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.is_marking
documentation:
`True` if in marking mode, `False` otherwise.
Marking mode means a mouse click adds a new marker.
This does not affect {meth}`add_markers`.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.start_marking
documentation:
Start marking, with option to name this set of markers or
to specify the marker style.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.stop_marking
documentation:
Stop marking mode, with option to clear markers, if desired.

# Parameters

clear_markers

: If `clear_markers` is `False`, existing markers are
  retained until {meth}`reset_markers` is called.
  Otherwise, they are erased.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.marker
astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.get_markers
documentation:
Return the locations of existing markers.

# Parameters

x_colname, y_colname

: Column names for X and Y data coordinates.
  Coordinates returned are 0- or 1-indexed, depending
  on `self.pixel_offset`.

skycoord_colname

: Column name for `SkyCoord`, which contains
  sky coordinates associated with the active image.
  This is ignored if image has no WCS.

# Returns

markers_table

: Table of markers, if any, or `None`.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget._validate_marker_name
documentation: Raise an error if the marker_name is not allowed.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.add_markers
documentation:
Creates markers in the image at given points.

```
.. todo::

    Later enhancements to include more columns
    to control size/style/color of marks,
```

# Parameters

table

: Table containing marker locations.

x_colname, y_colname

: Column names for X and Y.
  Coordinates can be 0- or 1-indexed, as
  given by `self.pixel_offset`.

skycoord_colname

: Column name with `SkyCoord` objects.

use_skycoord

: If `True`, use `skycoord_colname` to mark.
  Otherwise, use `x_colname` and `y_colname`.

marker_name

: Name to assign the markers in the table. Providing a name
  allows markers to be removed by name at a later time.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.remove_markers
documentation:
Remove some but not all of the markers by name used when
adding the markers

Parameters
----------

marker_name : str, optional
    Name used when the markers were added.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.reset_markers
documentation: Delete all markers.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.stretch_options
documentation: List all available options for image stretching.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.stretch
documentation: The image stretching algorithm in use.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.stretch
documentation: TODO: Possible to use astropy.visualization directly?

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.autocut_options
documentation: List all available options for image auto-cut.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.cuts
documentation:
Current image cut levels.
To set new cut levels, either provide a tuple of
`(low, high)` values or one of the options from
`autocut_options`.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.cuts
documentation: TODO: Possible to use astropy.visualization directly?

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.colormap_options
documentation: List of colormap names.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.set_colormap
documentation:
Set colormap to the given colormap name.

# Parameters

cmap

: Colormap name. Possible values can be obtained from
  {meth}`colormap_options`.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.cursor
documentation:
Show or hide cursor information (X, Y, WCS).
Acceptable values are 'top', 'bottom', or `None`.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.click_center
documentation:
Settable.
If True, middle-clicking can be used to center.  If False, that
interaction is disabled.

In the future this might go from True/False to being a selectable
button. But not for the first round.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.click_drag
documentation:
Settable.
If True, the "click-and-drag" mode is an available interaction for
panning.  If False, it is not.

Note that this should be automatically made `False` when selection mode
is activated.
TODO: Awaiting https://github.com/ejeschke/ginga/issues/674

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.scroll_pan
documentation:
Settable.
If True, scrolling moves around in the image.  If False, scrolling
(up/down) *zooms* the image in and out.

methods.name: astrowidgets.core.ImageWidget.save
documentation: Save out the current image view to given PNG filename.

Image widget for Jupyter notebook using Ginga viewer.

```
.. todo:: Any property passed to constructor has to be valid keyword.
```

# Parameters

logger

: Ginga logger. For example:

  ```
  from ginga.misc.log import get_logger
  logger = get_logger('my_viewer', log_stderr=False,
                      log_file='ginga.log', level=40)
  ```

image_width, image_height

: Dimension of Jupyter notebook's image widget.

use_opencv

: Let Ginga use `opencv` to speed up image transformation;
  e.g., rotation and mosaic. If this is enabled and you
  do not have `opencv`, you will get a warning.

pixel_coords_offset

: An offset, typically either 0 or 1, to add/subtract to all
  pixel values when going to/from the displayed image.
  *In almost all situations the default value, \`\`0\`\`, is the
  correct value to use.*
